mission.title: clusternet: cannot deploy demo application, hub and agent have some error logs
mission.description: cannot deploy demo application, hub and agent have some error logs. This issue affects 0+ users.

steps.title: Identify clusternet troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your clusternet deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=clusternet
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusternet -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `that is not an metav1.Status: &errors.errorString{s:"http2: stream closed"}: http2: stream closed`

steps.title: Check current Deployment configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant clusternet resources:
```bash
kubectl get deployment -A
kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>
```
#### What happened:

I use `hack/local-running.sh` to create one parent cluster and three child clusters. But I cannot deploy demo application, there is nothing happened.

#### What you expected to ha

steps.title: Apply the fix for cannot deploy demo application, hub and agent have some erro
steps.description:
Fixes clusternet/clusternet#656
```yaml
kubectl clusternet get ns
```

steps.title: Confirm cannot deploy demo application, hub and agent have is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusternet -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `that is not an metav1.Status: &errors.errorString{s:"http2: stream closed"}: http2: stream closed` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: This issue was resolved by applying the fix from the linked PR. The root cause was identified and addressed by the community.
codeSnippets:
subscribers: # defines the clusters to be distributed to
        - clusterAffinity:
            matchLabels:
              clusters.clusternet.io/cluster-id: dc91021d-2361-4f6d-a404-7c33b9e01118 # PLEASE UPDATE THIS CLUSTER-ID TO YOURS!!!

kubectl get base -A
kubectl get desc -A
kubectl describe desc -n clusternet-xxx sub-xxx

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with clusternet installed or the issue environment reproducible.
